briefdescription.para: DistInit initializes the distributor of the GIC.

listitem.para: Write the trigger mode, priority and target CPU

briefdescription.para: CPUInit initializes the CPU Interface of the GIC.

listitem.para: Set the priority of the CPU.

briefdescription.para: Looks up the device configuration based on the CPU interface base address of the device.

detaileddescription.para: A table contains the configuration info for each device in the system.

parameterdescription.para: CPU Interface Register base address.

simplesect.para: A pointer to the configuration structure for the specified device, or NULL if the device was not found.

briefdescription.para: Initializes the GIC based on the device ID.

parameterdescription.para: Device id to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This function is the primary interrupt handler for the driver.

detaileddescription.para: It must be connected to the interrupt source such that it is called when an interrupt of the interrupt controller is active. It will resolve which interrupts are active and enabled and call the appropriate interrupt handler. It uses the Interrupt Type information to determine when to acknowledge the interrupt. Highest priority interrupts are serviced first.
This function assumes that an interrupt vector table has been previously initialized. It does not verify that entries in the table are valid before calling an interrupt handler.

parameterdescription.para: Unique identifier for the ScuGic device.

memberdef.argsstring: (UINTPTR BaseAddress, s32 InterruptID, Xil_InterruptHandler IntrHandler, void *CallBackRef)
memberdef.name: XScuGic_RegisterHandler

briefdescription.para: Register a handler function for a specific interrupt ID.

detaileddescription.para: The vector table of the interrupt controller is updated, overwriting any previous handler. The handler function will be called when an interrupt occurs for the given interrupt ID.

parameterdescription.para: CPU Interface Register base address of the interrupt controller whose vector table will be modified.
Interrupt ID to be associated with the input handler.
Function pointer that will be added to the vector table for the given interrupt ID.
Argument that will be passed to the new handler function when it is called. This is user-specific.

simplesect.para: This function has no effect if the input base address is invalid.

memberdef.argsstring: (UINTPTR DistBaseAddress, u32 Int_Id, u8 Priority, u8 Trigger)
memberdef.name: XScuGic_SetPriTrigTypeByDistAddr

briefdescription.para: Sets the interrupt priority and trigger type for the specificd IRQ source.

parameterdescription.para: Distributor base address
IRQ source number to modify
New priority for the IRQ source. 0 is highest priority, 0xF8(248) is lowest. There are 32 priority levels supported with a step of 8. Hence the supported priorities are 0, 8, 16, 32, 40 ..., 248.

New trigger type for the IRQ source. Each bit pair describes the configuration for an INT_ID. SFI Read Only b10 always PPI Read Only depending on how the PPIs are configured. b01 Active HIGH level sensitive b11 Rising edge sensitive SPI LSB is read only. b01 Active HIGH level sensitive b11 Rising edge sensitive/

simplesect.para: This API has the similar functionality of XScuGic_SetPriority TriggerType() and should be used when there is no InstancePtr.

briefdescription.para: Gets the interrupt priority and trigger type for the specificd IRQ source.

parameterdescription.para: Distributor base address
IRQ source number to modify
Pointer to the value of the priority of the IRQ source. This is a return value.
Pointer to the value of the trigger of the IRQ source. This is a return value.

simplesect.para: This API has the similar functionality of XScuGic_GetPriority TriggerType() and should be used when there is no InstancePtr.

briefdescription.para: Sets the target CPU for the interrupt of a peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: Device base address
CPU number from which the interrupt has to be unmapped
IRQ source number to modify

briefdescription.para: Unmaps specific SPI interrupt from the target CPU.

parameterdescription.para: Device base address
CPU number from which the interrupt has to be unmapped
IRQ source number to modify

briefdescription.para: Unmaps all SPI interrupts from the target CPU.

parameterdescription.para: Device base address
CPU number from which the interrupts has to be unmapped

briefdescription.para: Enables the interrupt source provided as the argument Int_Id.

detaileddescription.para: Any pending interrupt condition for the specified Int_Id will occur after this function is called.

parameterdescription.para: Contains the ID of the interrupt source and should be in the range of 0 to XSCUGIC_MAX_NUM_INTR_INPUTS - 1.

briefdescription.para: Disables the interrupt source provided as the argument Int_Id so that the interrupt controller will not cause interrupts for the specified Int_Id.

detaileddescription.para: The interrupt controller will continue to hold an interrupt condition for the Int_Id, but will not cause an interrupt.

parameterdescription.para: Contains the ID of the interrupt source and should be in the range of 0 to XSCUGIC_MAX_NUM_INTR_INPUTS - 1.
